folio.label: 175r
folio.l: perils , æ ben in werre ,

and seide to hem in goodly manere ,
how æ hem ougúten haue gret repentau~ce ,

of the iniuries and
wronges æ they hadden doon to Melibe hire lord , & vn to hire
& to hire dougúter

¢ And whan they herden , the goodly wor-
des of Dame Pèdence ,

they weren so supprised and rauysshed
& hadden so gret ioye of hire , æ wonder was to telle

¢ A
lady q¶ they , ©e han shewed vn to vs , the blissynge of swet-
nesse , after the lawe of Dauid the ¸phete ,

for the reco~silynge
which we be nat worthy to haue , in no manere ,

but we ough-
ten requeren it , w gret contécion & humilitee ,

©e of ©oure
grete goodnesse , haue päsented vn to vs

¢ Now se we wel , æ
the science & the konnynge of Salamon is ful trewe ,

For he
seitú , æ swete wordes multiplien & encressen frendes , And
maken shrewes to be debonaire & meke

¢ Certes q¶ æei , we
putten oure dede , and al oure matere & cause , al holly in
©oure good wyl ,

and ben redy to obeye , vn to the speche , and
comaundement , of my lord Melibe ,

And æäfore , deere & benyg-
ne lady , we preye ©ow & biseke ©ow , as mekely as we konne &
moun ,

that it like vn to ©oåe gret goodnesse , to fulfille i~ dede
©oure goodly wordes ,

For we co~sideren & knowelichen , æ we
han offended , & greuyd my lord Melibe , out of mesure ,

so fer-
fortú , æ we ben nat of power , to maken his amendes ,

And
æäfore we oblige vs , & bynde vs & oure frendes , for to do aÂ
his wil & hise comau~dementz ,

but ¹auenture , he hatú swicú
heuynesse , & swicú wrathe to vs ward , by cause of oure offence

æ he wol enioyne vs swicú a peyne , as we moun nat bere ,
ne sustene ,

And æäfore noble lady , we biseke , to ©oure wo~man-
ly pitee ,

to taken swicú a vysement in this nede , æ we ne
oure frendes , ben nat desherited & destroyed thurgú oure fo-
lie

¢ Certes quod Pèdence , it is an hard thyngö and rigút
¹ilous ,

æ a man putte hi~ al outrely in the arbitraco~n &
Iuggement , & in the mygút & powere of hise enemys ,,

¢ For Salamou~ seitú ; leeuetú me , & ©euetú credence , to æ
that I shal seyn , I seye q¶ he , ©e peeple folk & gouänours
of holy chirche ,

to thy sone , to thy wyf , to thy frend , ne
to thy brother ,

ne ©eue æè neuäe mygút ne maistrie of æi
body , whil æè lyuest

by a strengere resou~ , he deffendetú and forbe-
detú a man , to ©eue hi~ self to his enemy ,

And nathelees
I counseille ©ow , æ ©e mys truste nat my lord ,

For I woot
wel & knowe verraily , æ he is debonaire & meke , large , Cur-
teys ,
& no thyngö desirous ne coueitous of good ne richesse ,
